headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 release dominated the week with a wave of agentic demonstrations.
body: Anthropic released the Mythos model family alongside Fable 5, described as Mythos 5 with guardrails, offered at no extra cost on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22 before shifting to usage credits. A cluster of demonstrations showed the model acting as an autonomous agent: playing Factorio, simulating the solar system to predict eclipses, generating fluid simulations set to Beethoven, and building a CAD editor with a copilot to design 3D-printable models. On the infrastructure side, new entrants in distributed training surfaced including context parallelism, fully sharded data parallelism, and auto-scaling, with RunPod showing IDE-based GPU deployment at $0.00116 per second. Training-free methods and AI engineering also appeared as fresh technique entrants.

cited_items.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
